_notice: ALL RECORDS BELOW ARE FICTIONAL. This file illustrates the STRUCTURE of the Stripe-to-Dataverse reconciliation model (payout->Deposit, charge->Transaction, donor matching, and exceptions). It contains no production data, no real donor, no real Stripe object, and no real amount. Every id, name, and value was invented for demonstration.
_evidence_tier: Invented sample. Field concepts reconstruct the documented model; values are fictional. Sensitive Stripe fields are represented only by generalized placeholders, never real values.

deposits.note: Fictional Deposit created from a fictional Stripe payout. Reference is a placeholder, not a real Stripe payout id.

donor_match_examples.note: Fictional. Illustrates matching by a stored processor-customer link. No real ids.
Fictional. Email normalization (trim + lowercase) is shown as a concept only; no real email value is stored here.
Fictional. Illustrates a secondary identifying signal. Exact tier order is not preserved and is not invented.

exception_examples.note: Fictional exception record. Represents the manual-review path, not production data.
Fictional. Ambiguous matches leave the automatic path by design.
Fictional. A charge whose payout has not yet settled cannot bind to a Deposit.
